title: Lemma 2.5: prove $M\not\preceq_M B$ via $\|E_B(x(u_1u_2)^ny)\|_2\to0$ and contrapositive of Popa intertwining

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** Proof of Lemma 2.5, parenthetical "(as $\|E_B(x(u_1u_2)^n y)\|_2\xrightarrow{n\to\infty}0$ for all $x,y\in M$)".

The proof needs $M\not\preceq_M B$ to contradict the consequence of [OP10b, Lemma 3.3]/[BH18, Thm 2] that an $M$-central positive functional on $\mathbb B(L^2M)$ non-vanishing on $\overline{Me_BM}$ forces $M\preceq_M B$. The justification is an unproved mixing estimate.

**Required:** prove $\|E_B((u_1u_2)^n)\|_2\to0$ (and the two-sided version with $x,y$) from the AFP reduced-word structure — with $E_B(u_1)=E_B(u_2)=E_B(u_1^*u_2)=0$ the alternating word $u_1u_2u_1u_2\cdots$ stays reduced of growing length, giving asymptotic $B$-orthogonality — and then invoke the precise statement (and negation) of Popa's intertwining theorem to conclude $M\not\preceq_M B$.
